primary_sources.relevance: GitHub documents exp, iat, jti, nbf and identity-bearing workflow claims; temporal calibration must not persist the latter.
Defines NumericDate temporal claims and JWT claim semantics.
Supports explicit validation and restrictive processing rather than deriving trust from token structure alone.

primary_sources.status: current official privacy-engineering program page inspected 2026-07-13
primary_sources.relevance: Supports minimizing retained data and separating operational measurement from identity-bearing records.

finding: R-008 safely emits redacted temporal observations, but policy calibration still needs a second boundary: aggregate only after a minimum cohort, reject raw-token and identity-bearing fields, retain aggregate temporal statistics only, and never authorize a verifier policy change automatically.
design_implication: Cloudflare workflows can collect independently verified R-008 observations and pass them to the R-009 aggregator. The result is descriptive evidence for human policy review, not an automatic widening of token lifetime or clock-skew acceptance.

cases: identity-bearing and raw-token fields are rejected
verification.method: Tests ran before commit against byte-identical files in an isolated Node runtime.

uncertainty: No live GitHub OIDC observations were sampled. The default cohort threshold of five is a conservative engineering minimum, not a statistically universal guarantee.
falsification_route: Collect a larger independently verified cohort across workflow runs. Reject the calibration if later observations exceed the reported envelope, if samples are dependent, or if provider documentation changes claim semantics.
